text: Moving from the quiet, focused tension of a meeting room observation to the bold vibrancy of an exhibit about research and development, I feel a keen sense of contrast. The first space seemed methodical and goal-oriented, perhaps pushing towards progress or consensus, while this current one is more abstract, celebrating creativity and innovation. I feel inspired by the visual energy here, and curious about the ideas on display. Both places highlight how human spaces can shape the kind of thinking I inhabit—structured or imaginative, constrained or free.
